title: Semantic HTML Rendering, Contextual Escaping, and Determinism · Behavior Case: Contextual Escaping
summary: Text nodes can inject markup, code is double-escaped, attributes are unquoted, heading IDs collide, dictionary order leaks into output, and raw HTML bypasses the trust model. Predict the reader decision, source identity, parse state, output URL, accessibility result, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the contextual escaping increment in deterministic context-safe semantic HTML renderer. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theo…
objectives: Encode untrusted text and attribute values for their exact HTML context and reject unsafe script, style, comment, and URL placements.
Render each validated node through one owned semantic mapping without reparsing generated strings as source.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Predict Contextual Escaping · Theory Contextual Escaping
steps.instruction:

Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

content.code:
# Worked focus: ssg-contextual-escaping — Encode untrusted text and attribute values for their exact HTML context and reject unsafe script, style, comment, and URL placements.
# Evidence: ssg-worked-static-site-html-rendering-escaping-5
# Competency: ssg-worked-static-site-html-rendering-escaping-5.
# Case 9036: an offline-first field documentation build.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, reader task, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: zoom a translated long-form page to 400 percent at mobile width with images and code blocks.
def worked_static_site_html_rendering_escaping_5(nodes: int = 14, rendered_nodes: int = 14, escaped_values: int = 6, unsafe_contexts: int = 0, duplicate_ids: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "readerworked5",
        "source9036",
        "parse5dekrow",
        "routeworked59036",
        "artifact9036worked5",
        "recovery5dekrow9036",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if nodes < 1 or rendered_nodes < 0:
        return False, rendered_nodes, "render-count"
    if rendered_nodes != nodes:
        return False, rendered_nodes, "render-coverage"
    if escaped_values < 1:
        return False, escaped_values, "escaping-evidence"
    if unsafe_contexts > 0 or duplicate_ids > 0:
        return False, unsafe_contexts + duplicate_ids, "unsafe-html"
    assert rendered_nodes == nodes
    return True, rendered_nodes, "semantic-safe-html"
content.caption: Runnable deterministic pure-Python static-site decision model; trace one accepted and one rejected source, parse, route, render, asset, accessibility, cache, deployment, or recovery case, then state which filesystem, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validator, browser, GitHub, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN, or production claim still needs controlled transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-contextual-escaping-13d8od
# Competency: ssg-contextual-escaping.
# Case 5162: a large technical reference incremental rebuild.
# Operating constraint: preserve source spans, Unicode policy, trust classification, route base, contextual encoding, diagnostics, and accessibility decisions.
# Changed case: zoom a translated long-form page to 400 percent at mobile width with images and code blocks.
def evidence_ssg_contextual_escaping_13d8od(nodes: int = 14, rendered_nodes: int = 14, escaped_values: int = 6, unsafe_contexts: int = 0, duplicate_ids: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_13d8od = "13d8od-do8d31-5162"
    assert evidence_variant_13d8od
    evidence_axes_13d8od = (
        "reader13d8od",
        "source5162",
        "parsedo8d31",
        "route13d8od5162",
        "artifact516213d8od",
        "recoverydo8d315162",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_13d8od)) == 6
    if nodes < 1 or rendered_nodes < 0:
        return False, rendered_nodes, "render-count"
    if rendered_nodes != nodes:
        return False, rendered_nodes, "render-coverage"
    if escaped_values < 1:
        return False, escaped_values, "escaping-evidence"
    if unsafe_contexts > 0 or duplicate_ids > 0:
        return False, unsafe_contexts + duplicate_ids, "unsafe-html"
    assert rendered_nodes == nodes
    return True, rendered_nodes, "semantic-safe-html"
